title: Public object storage to sensitive data exposure

steps.action: Identify publicly accessible bucket and enumerate exposed objects within authorized scope.
steps.goal: Confirm whether storage is exposed to anonymous access.

steps.action: Review exposed files for sensitive business or configuration data.
steps.goal: Determine exposure impact without exceeding scope.

detections: access logs showing anonymous object retrieval
remediation: Restrict bucket policy to approved principals
Review exposed objects and rotate any exposed secrets

title: Public storage exposure to credential discovery and privilege escalation risk
initial_condition: Blob container is publicly accessible and contains deployment artifacts

steps.action: Validate anonymous access to storage container within authorized scope.
Inspect exposed artifacts for credentials, tokens, or infrastructure references without reproducing secrets in outputs.
steps.goal: Assess whether exposure could enable further compromise.

steps.action: Model potential privilege escalation paths from exposed cloud credentials or deployment identities.
steps.goal: Estimate blast radius and control impact.

detections: public access alerts on storage account
remediation: Review RBAC assignments and apply least privilege
Implement storage scanning for secret exposure

title: Internet-exposed administrative access surface
initial_condition: EC2 instance has port 22 open to 0.0.0.0/0

steps.action: Identify public administrative access rules and internet-facing host context.
Model likely credential attack and remote access scenarios without attempting destructive or out-of-scope activity.
steps.goal: Estimate practical exposure and threat relevance.
evidence: admin service banner or configuration clues

impact: Higher brute force and credential attack risk
remediation: Restrict SSH to approved management IP ranges
Use bastion architecture or zero-trust admin access
Continuously detect drift in security group rules

title: Overly permissive IAM enabling privilege escalation paths
initial_condition: IAM role allows broad actions and resources beyond workload function

steps.action: Review IAM permissions and trust relationships for workload role.
steps.goal: Identify excessive privileges and abuse opportunities.

steps.action: Model how compromised workload credentials could access additional resources or escalate privileges.
steps.goal: Estimate blast radius from identity misuse.

remediation: Alert on policy drift and privileged API usage
